meta.importedFrom: Unit 4 Passage 2 The time-eating monster（互动课文教学动画）英语新教材外研版七年级下册.html

article.title: The time-eating monster
article.unit: Unit 4 The art of having fun
article.summary: 识别吞噬时间的娱乐陷阱，学习保持有节制的快乐。

sentences.text: Have you ever noticed a monster around us?
sentences.tr: 你有没有注意到我们身边有一个怪兽？
sentences.d: Have you ever done...? 意为“你曾经做过...吗？”

sentences.text: It's not big or scary, but it's very dangerous.
sentences.tr: 它并不高大也不可怕，但它非常危险。
sentences.d: but 连接两个分句，表示转折。

sentences.text: This monster comes in many forms – games, social media and TV shows.
sentences.tr: 这个怪兽以多种形式出现——游戏、社交媒体和电视节目。
sentences.d: come in many forms 以多种形式出现。

sentences.text: You may ask, “It brings us so much fun.”
sentences.tr: 你可能会问：“它带给我们这么多乐趣。”
sentences.d: may 表示推测；bring sb. sth. 给某人带来某物。

sentences.text: So why do you say it’s a monster?
sentences.tr: 那你为什么说它是怪兽呢？
sentences.d: why 引导特殊疑问句，询问原因。

sentences.text: At first, we may all think, “I’ll just have a little fun.”
sentences.tr: 起初，我们可能都会想：“我只是想玩一会儿。”
sentences.d: At first 意为“起初”；have a little fun 玩一会儿。

sentences.text: We turn on the computer and start a game.
sentences.tr: 我们打开电脑，开始玩游戏。
sentences.d: turn on 意为“打开（电器等）”。

sentences.text: But soon the monster takes over.
sentences.tr: 但很快怪兽就接管了。
sentences.d: take over 意为“接管，控制”。

sentences.text: It starts to eat away our time, minute by minute.
sentences.tr: 它开始一分钟一分钟地吞噬我们的时间。
sentences.d: start to do sth. 开始做某事；minute by minute 一分钟一分钟地。

sentences.text: But we just sit there and look at the screen without noticing it at all.
sentences.tr: 但我们只是坐在那里盯着屏幕，完全没有注意到它。
sentences.d: without doing sth. 意为“没有做某事”；not...at all 一点也不。

sentences.text: We often get lost in the monster’s world like this.
sentences.tr: 我们就这样经常迷失在怪兽的世界里。
sentences.d: get lost 意为“迷失”。

sentences.text: We become couch potatoes with sore necks and dry eyes.
sentences.tr: 我们变成了脖子酸痛、眼睛干涩的“电视迷”。
sentences.d: couch potato 意为“整天坐在沙发上看电视的人”。

sentences.text: We miss out on sweet moments with loved ones.
sentences.tr: 我们错过了与亲人在一起的甜蜜时刻。
sentences.d: miss out on 意为“错过”。

sentences.text: We are deaf to the real world.
sentences.tr: 我们对现实世界充耳不闻。
sentences.d: be deaf to 意为“对...充耳不闻”。

sentences.text: Just remember, too much fun also makes Jack a dull boy!
sentences.tr: 记住，过度娱乐也会让孩子变傻！
sentences.d: too much 意为“太多”。

sentences.text: The time-eating monster will always be there.
sentences.tr: 时间怪兽永远都在那里。
sentences.d: will always be 意为“将永远是”。

sentences.text: But we can keep it away by having fun in a balanced way.
sentences.tr: 但我们可以通过平衡的娱乐方式让它远离。
sentences.d: keep...away 意为“使...远离”；by doing sth. 通过做某事。

sentences.text: So, stand up and leave the screens!
sentences.tr: 所以，站起来，离开屏幕！
sentences.d: stand up 站起来；leave 离开。

sentences.text: Go outside, feel the sun on your face, and jog in the fresh air.
sentences.tr: 去户外，感受阳光洒在脸上，在清新的空气中慢跑。
sentences.d: feel the sun 感受阳光；fresh air 清新的空气。

sentences.text: Play tennis or go hiking with your friends.
sentences.tr: 和朋友一起打网球或徒步旅行。
sentences.d: go hiking 去徒步；with your friends 和你的朋友。

sentences.text: Talk about your day with your family.
sentences.tr: 和家人聊聊你的一天。
sentences.d: talk about 谈论；your day 你的一天。

sentences.text: There is so much for you to do.
sentences.tr: 你有这么多事情可以做。
sentences.d: There is so much for you to do. 有很多事等着你去做。

sentences.text: The choice is ours.
sentences.tr: 选择权在我们手中。
sentences.d: ours 是名词性物主代词，意为“我们的”。

vocabItems.meaningZh: v. 注意到(notice的过去分词)
v. 它是 (it is的缩略词)
n. 形式，形状(form的名词复数)
v. 我将 (I will的缩略词)
adj. 迷失的(lose的过去分词)
adj. 被爱的(love的过去分词)
adj. 平衡的(balance的过去分词)
n. 徒步旅行(hike的动名词)
v. 不要 (do not的缩略词)
adj. 吞噬时间的，耗时的

readingQuestions.stem: What is the 'monster' mentioned in the text?
options: B. Games, social media and TV
C. A person who eats time
readingQuestions.answer: B. Games, social media and TV
readingQuestions.rationaleZh: 第一段明确提到：This monster comes in many forms – games, social media and TV shows.

readingQuestions.stem: What happens when the monster 'takes over'?
options: C. It eats away our time
D. We turn off the computer
readingQuestions.answer: C. It eats away our time
readingQuestions.rationaleZh: 第二段提到：But soon the monster takes over. It starts to eat away our time.

readingQuestions.stem: What does 'couch potato' mean in paragraph 3?
options: A. A potato on a couch
C. Someone who spends too much time watching TV
readingQuestions.answer: C. Someone who spends too much time watching TV
readingQuestions.rationaleZh: Couch potato 是英语习语，指整天坐在沙发上看电视的人。

readingQuestions.stem: How can we keep the monster away?
options: B. By having fun in a balanced way
D. By buying a new screen
readingQuestions.answer: B. By having fun in a balanced way
readingQuestions.rationaleZh: 第四段提到：But we can keep it away by having fun in a balanced way.

readingQuestions.stem: Which activity is NOT suggested in the last paragraph?
readingQuestions.rationaleZh: 最后一段建议离开屏幕去户外活动，而不是看更多电视。

noticed.meaningZh: v. 注意到(notice的过去分词)

it's.meaningZh: v. 它是 (it is的缩略词)

forms.meaningZh: n. 形式，形状(form的名词复数)

i'll.meaningZh: v. 我将 (I will的缩略词)

lost.meaningZh: adj. 迷失的(lose的过去分词)

loved.meaningZh: adj. 被爱的(love的过去分词)

balanced.meaningZh: adj. 平衡的(balance的过去分词)

hiking.meaningZh: n. 徒步旅行(hike的动名词)

don't.meaningZh: v. 不要 (do not的缩略词)

time-eating.meaningZh: adj. 吞噬时间的，耗时的
